reforms.description: A new interest rate subsidy scheme for 'innovative' investment, known as 'TOP-AKTION', was introduced to support businesses in making innovative investments. Additionally, the Federal Government announced an employment-creating package that includes higher interest rate subsidies for small businesses and agriculture.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The subsidies are designed to encourage innovative investments and support small businesses, which can enhance productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The interest rate subsidy scheme was implemented in 1981, while the employment-creating package was announced in 1982.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms introduce significant support mechanisms for innovation but do not represent a structural overhaul of the system.
reforms.source_quote: The Federal Government announced an employment-creating package comprising the following measures: higher interest rate subsidies for small business and agriculture.
